title: leea sambucina var. roehrsiana
intro: Leea sambucina var. roehrsiana is a plant species, commonly known as the Indian lilac or Leeabark, which belongs to the family Leeaceae. Traditionally, Leeabark has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Leeabark include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Leeabark can be made into teas, infusions, and ointments, and it is also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat a range of health issues, including skin conditions and infections.
